problem: Every June 1, an ecologist takes a census of the number of wrens in a state park. She noticed that the number is decreasing by $40\%$ each year. If this trend continues, in what year will the census show that the number of wrens is less than $10\%$ of what it was on June 1, 2004?

classified_questions.question: What is the initial percentage decrease in the number of wrens each year?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we express the decrease in terms of a percentage?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What will be the percentage of wrens remaining after one year?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the percentage of wrens remaining after two years?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is there a pattern in the percentages of wrens remaining after each year? If so, can you describe it?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Based on the pattern you observed, when will the percentage of wrens remaining be less than 10%?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: In what year will the census show that the number of wrens is less than 10% of what it was on June 1, 2004?
classified_questions.label: b
